channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Google reveals California’s favorite Easter candy
item.description: Everyone has their go-to Easter treat—be it chocolate eggs, jellybeans, or gummies—but a new Google Trends study shows which sweet was the favorite in each state. Peeps were found to be Californians’ top Easter candy choice.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Harvey Weinstein can stay in hospital during #MeToo retrial, judge rules
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Harvey Weinstein has been moved to a New York City hospital after a judge approved the ailing ex-studio boss’s request to stay there rather than in jail when he’s not in court for his #MeToo retrial.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Worshippers return to restored Notre Dame for Easter rituals with emotion and awe
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Four months after its long-awaited reopening, Notre Dame Cathedral welcomed worshippers and tourists alike on Friday for an emotional Easter ceremony centered on the Crown of Thorns, a circular band of branches encased in a golden tube and one of Christianity’s most revered relics.
item.dc:creator: TOM NOUVIAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: FDA hiring contractors to replace terminated safety inspection staffers
item.description: When Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. announced sweeping job cuts at his department last month, he said that safety inspectors who oversee U.S. foods and drugs wouldn't be impacted.
item.dc:creator: By MATTHEW PERRONE AP Health Writer, Fox 5

item.title: Karen Read prosecutor wants to block defense from blaming after-party guests, pet dog in opening statement
item.description: Prosecution motion filed in Karen Read case to limit defense strategy to blame law enforcement or dog for Boston Police Officer John O'Keefe's death.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump admin to cut 90% of CFPB in latest layoffs: Reports
item.description: President Donald Trump’s administration is cutting nearly 90 percent of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau’s (CFPB) employees in a push to significantly downsize the watchdog agency formed to shield U.S. consumers from financial fraud and abuse.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: More details emerge about Florida State University shooting suspect Phoenix Ikner as motive remains a mystery
item.description: More details are emerging Friday about Florida State University shooting suspect Phoenix Ikner as law enforcement is trying to determine the motive of the attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court to hear rare May arguments in birthright citizenship battle
item.description: The Supreme Court scheduled a rare May oral argument on the Trump administration’s emergency request to narrow a series of nationwide blocks on President Trump’s executive order that would restrict birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Arson attack probe at Pennsylvania governor's mansion looking into suspect's hatred of Josh Shapiro
item.description: HERSHEY, Pa. (AP) — Authorities investigating why a man set fire to Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro’s official residence are probing whether the suspect was motivated by the Democrat's Jewish faith or positions on Israel’s war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Red Robin launches $20 Bottomless Burger Pass for National Burger Month
item.description: May is National Burger Month and Red Robin is celebrating with the launch of its Bottomless Burger Pass — a limited-time offer that gives fans a month of daily gourmet burgers and bottomless sides for just $20. The pass will be available for purchase beginning April 17.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Actor Haley Joel Osment booked for 'public intoxication' at California ski resort
item.description: Actor Haley Joel Osment, famous for his role as the child who saw “dead people” in the 1999 thriller “The Sixth Sense” starring Bruce Willis, was reportedly arrested at a ski resort in Mammoth Lakes for public intoxication, according to multiple media reports.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WGN 9

item.title: Two dead and 6 injured in Florida State shooting; gunman is deputy's son, police say
item.description: Authorities identified the suspected shooter as a 20-year-old student who is also the son of a local sheriff's deputy.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, WGN 9

item.title: Obama, Sanders, Ben Carson among most liked politicians, surveys show
item.description: Former President Barack Obama, Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.) and former Secretary of Housing and Urban Development Ben Carson are among the most liked politicians, according to new YouGov surveys.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: Officials in Puerto Rico demand answers after another blackout leaves 1.4 million without power
item.description: Crews worked early Thursday to restore power to Puerto Rico after a blackout across the entire island that affected the main international airport, several hospitals and hotels filled with Easter vacationers.
item.dc:creator: Dánica Coto, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Republicans consider increasing taxes on the rich in break from party orthodoxy
item.description: Republicans in Congress are considering increasing taxes on the rich as a part of President Trump’s “big, beautiful bill” of ambitious legislative priorities, a striking development that breaks with decades of party orthodoxy and is spurring alarm bells from traditional conservatives.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Handyman named person of interest after missing woman's body found buried in concrete
item.description: A missing woman was found buried and partially encased in concrete more than a week after she went missing. Now, deputies are questioning her handyman for his possible role in her disappearance and death.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KTLA 5

item.title: Proposed rule change on endangered species triggers alarm for environmentalists
item.description: The Trump administration plans to eliminate habitat protections for endangered and threatened species in a move environmentalists say would lead to the extinction of critically endangered species because of logging, mining, development and other activities.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NY prison guards beat an inmate to death then tried to cover it up, prosecutors say
item.description: UTICA, N.Y. (AP) — Ten New York prison guards were charged Wednesday in connection with the fatal beating of a 22-year-old inmate last month — including two charged with murder. It's the second time a group of correctional officers in the state was indicted for a death behind bars this year.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL and DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: AmeriCorps members who respond to disasters and help nonprofits are let go in DOGE cuts
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Young volunteers who respond to natural disasters and help with community projects across the U.S. have been discharged as a result of the Trump administration 's campaign to shrink government workforce and services.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration issues order to stop construction on New York offshore wind project
item.description: The Trump administration issued an order Wednesday to stop construction on a major offshore wind project to power more than 500,000 New York homes, the latest in a series of moves targeting the industry.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lost toddler found miles from home by dog that guided him to safety, sheriff says
item.description: When an Arizona toddler wandered away from home Monday, a team of deputies, search and rescue volunteers and rangers began their search. In the end, it was a protective pup who track down the 2-year-old.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WGN 9

item.title: St. Pete woman reunites with nurse who saved her life after crash
item.description: A Pinellas County woman got the chance to reunite with the nurse who helped save her life after a crash two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Marino, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Handyman named person of interest after missing woman's body is found buried in concrete
item.description: A missing woman was found buried and partially encased in concrete nearly two weeks after she went missing. Now, deputies are questioning her handyman for his role in her disappearance and death.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 3 dead in multi-boat crash during Major League Fishing tournament in Alabama
item.description: A fatal boat crash during a Major League Fishing tournament in Alabama is currently under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Austin Franklin, ABC 4

item.title: Student accused in Dallas school shooting walked toward group in hall and opened fire, injuring 4
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — The student accused of injuring four in a shooting at a Dallas high school was let into the building through an unsecured door and then walked down a hallway toward a group of students, opening fire on them and then appearing to take a point-blank shot at one, according to an arrest
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: ACLU sues Defense Department school system over banning race- and gender-related books
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union has sued the Department of Defense’s school system for children of military families, asserting that the removal of race- and gender-related books and curricula violated students’ First Amendment protections against government censorship.
item.dc:creator: OLIVIA DIAZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Boasberg moves to hold Trump administration in contempt over deportation flights
item.description: A federal judge found probable cause Wednesday to hold the Trump administration in criminal contempt for willfully disobeying his order to immediately halt deportations under the rarely used Alien Enemies Act and turn around any airborne planes. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, ABC 4

item.title: AP Was There: A truck bomb rips through a federal building in Oklahoma City in 1995
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — EDITORS NOTE: On April 19, 1995, a former U.S. Army soldier parked a rented Ryder truck loaded with a powerful bomb made of fertilizer and fuel oil outside a federal office building in Oklahoma City. The blast at the Alfred P.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: White House removes wire spot from press pool
item.description: The White House is making changes to which outlets are included in the press pool covering President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: New Jersey Transit train engineers reject labor deal with management
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (AP) — New Jersey Transit train engineers have rejected a labor agreement with management, raising the potential for a strike or a lockout next month.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wink Martindale, the genial game show host and an early TV interviewer of Elvis Presley, dies at 91
item.description: Wink Martindale, the genial host of such hit game shows as “Gambit” and “Tic-Tac-Dough” who also did one of the first recorded television interviews with a young Elvis Presley, has died. He was 91.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Santa Fe sheriff releases body camera footage of Gene Hackman investigation
item.description: Newly-released body camera footage shows the confusion and questions investigators had after actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa were found dead in their home.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Upchurch, WGN 9

item.title: Man arrested outside UnitedHealthcare is now charged with firearms counts and threatening violence
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A man who was arrested outside the headquarters of UnitedHealthcare had a history of mental health issues and threatened to shoot up corporate facilities, according to charges filed Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why every MLB player will wear the same number Tuesday
item.description: Every MLB player, regardless of team or position, will be wearing the exact same jersey number on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Go inside the factory where Peeps are made
item.description: BETHLEHEM, Pa. (AP) — Love them or hate them, those marshmallow Peeps that come in blindingly bright colors and an array of flavors are inescapable around the Easter holiday.
item.dc:creator: TASSANEE VEJPONGSA and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal judge drops contempt case against ICE agent over arrest outside Boston courthouse
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge has dismissed a contempt case against an agent for the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement whose arrest of a man during his trial sparked criticism among the law enforcement community in Boston.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jeffries: Court should hold Trump officials in contempt over wrongly deported Maryland man
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) said Tuesday that the Supreme Court should hold members of the Trump administration in contempt of court if they don’t move to return a Maryland man mistakenly deported last month to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, WGN 9
